terms.name: Larghissimo
terms.definition: Extremely slow, slower than largo, typically below 20 BPM

terms.name: Largo
terms.definition: Very slow and broad, between 40-60 BPM

terms.name: Lento
terms.definition: Slow, similar to largo but less solemn

terms.name: Grave
terms.definition: Very slow, solemn, and serious in character

terms.name: Adagietto
terms.definition: Slightly faster than adagio, moderately slow

terms.name: Andante
terms.definition: Walking pace, moderate tempo around 76-108 BPM

terms.name: Andantino
terms.definition: Slightly faster than andante, or sometimes slower

terms.name: Moderato
terms.definition: Moderate tempo, neither fast nor slow

terms.name: Allegretto
terms.definition: Moderately fast, slightly slower than allegro

terms.name: Allegro
terms.definition: Fast, quick, and bright, around 120-168 BPM

terms.name: Vivacissimo
terms.definition: Very fast and lively, faster than vivace

terms.name: Prestissimo
terms.definition: As fast as possible, above 200 BPM

terms.name: BPM
terms.definition: Beats Per Minute, the standard unit for measuring tempo

terms.name: half-time
terms.definition: Tempo feel that halves the perceived pulse, drums play at half speed

terms.name: double-time
terms.definition: Tempo feel that doubles the perceived pulse, creating urgency

terms.name: free time
terms.definition: No fixed tempo, performer has complete rhythmic freedom

terms.name: rubato
terms.definition: Flexible tempo with expressive speeding up and slowing down

terms.name: a tempo
terms.definition: Return to the original tempo after a deviation

terms.name: ad libitum
terms.definition: At liberty, performer chooses tempo and interpretation freely

terms.name: tempo giusto
terms.definition: In strict time, exact and precise tempo

terms.name: swing feel
terms.definition: Rhythmic interpretation where eighth notes are played unevenly

terms.name: straight feel
terms.definition: Rhythmic interpretation with even, precise subdivisions

terms.name: laid-back
terms.definition: Relaxed feel, playing slightly behind the beat

terms.name: pushing
terms.definition: Urgent feel, playing slightly ahead of the beat

terms.name: on the beat
terms.definition: Precise rhythmic placement exactly on the pulse

terms.name: behind the beat
terms.definition: Relaxed placement slightly after the main pulse

terms.name: ahead of the beat
terms.definition: Urgent placement slightly before the main pulse

terms.name: pocket
terms.definition: The optimal groove where all elements lock together perfectly

terms.name: elastic tempo
terms.definition: Flexible timing that breathes with the music

terms.name: human feel
terms.definition: Natural timing variations that add expression

terms.name: time signature
terms.definition: Notational convention specifying beats per measure and note value
Common time, four quarter notes per measure
Waltz time, three quarter notes per measure
Compound duple meter, six eighth notes grouped in two
Quintuple meter, five quarter notes per measure
Septuple meter, seven eighth notes per measure
Compound triple meter, nine eighth notes grouped in three
Compound quadruple meter, twelve eighth notes in four groups

terms.name: compound meter
terms.definition: Meter where beats divide into three parts

terms.name: simple meter
terms.definition: Meter where beats divide into two parts

terms.name: mixed meter
terms.definition: Changing time signatures within a piece

terms.name: hemiola
terms.definition: Rhythmic pattern implying a shift between duple and triple meter

terms.name: tuplets
terms.definition: Irregular rhythmic groupings within standard meter

terms.name: triplets
terms.definition: Three notes played in the space of two

terms.name: quintuplets
terms.definition: Five notes played in the space of four

terms.name: septuplets
terms.definition: Seven notes played in the space of four or eight

terms.name: metric modulation
terms.definition: Smooth tempo change using a common note value

terms.name: isorhythm
terms.definition: Medieval technique with repeating rhythmic and pitch patterns

terms.name: subtractive rhythm
terms.definition: Rhythms created by removing notes from patterns

terms.name: whole note
terms.definition: Four-beat note duration, longest standard value

terms.name: half note
terms.definition: Two-beat note duration, half of whole note

terms.name: quarter note
terms.definition: One-beat note duration, standard pulse unit

terms.name: dotted note
terms.definition: Note with added half its value

terms.name: tied note
terms.definition: Two notes connected, durations added together

terms.name: syncopation
terms.definition: Accent on normally weak beats or off-beats

terms.name: anticipation
terms.definition: Playing a note slightly before the expected beat

terms.name: displacement
terms.definition: Shifting a rhythmic pattern forward or backward

terms.name: on-beat
terms.definition: Notes falling directly on the main pulses

terms.name: off-beat
terms.definition: Notes falling between the main pulses

terms.name: backbeat
terms.definition: Accent on beats 2 and 4 in 4/4 time

terms.name: downbeat
terms.definition: First beat of a measure, strongest accent

terms.name: upbeat
terms.definition: Last beat of a measure leading to the downbeat

terms.name: anacrusis
terms.definition: Notes before the first downbeat, pickup notes

terms.name: pickup
terms.definition: Same as anacrusis, notes leading into first full measure

terms.name: funky
terms.definition: Rhythmically infectious with strong backbeat and syncopation

terms.name: straight
terms.definition: Even, precise rhythmic interpretation without swing

terms.name: swung
terms.definition: Uneven eighth-note feel with long-short pattern

terms.name: shuffled
terms.definition: Similar to swung, with pronounced triplet feel

terms.name: breathable
terms.definition: Natural, human timing with subtle variations

terms.name: elastic
terms.definition: Flexible timing that stretches and compresses

terms.name: ppp (pianississimo)
terms.definition: Extremely soft, as quiet as possible

terms.name: fff (fortississimo)
terms.definition: Extremely loud, as loud as possible

terms.name: sforzando
terms.definition: Sudden strong accent on a note or chord

terms.name: terraced dynamics
terms.definition: Baroque practice of abrupt dynamic changes

terms.name: hairpin
terms.definition: Visual symbol for crescendo or diminuendo

terms.name: sub-accent
terms.definition: Slight emphasis, lighter than full accent

terms.name: loud
terms.definition: High amplitude, strong presence in mix

terms.name: punchy
terms.definition: Sharp attacks with strong transient presence

terms.name: wall of sound
terms.definition: Dense, layered production style pioneered by Phil Spector

terms.name: wide dynamic range
terms.definition: Large difference between softest and loudest parts

terms.name: narrow dynamic range
terms.definition: Small difference between softest and loudest parts

terms.name: major
terms.definition: Scale with W-W-H-W-W-W-H pattern, bright and stable sound

terms.name: natural minor
terms.definition: Scale with W-H-W-W-H-W-W pattern, melancholic sound

terms.name: harmonic minor
terms.definition: Minor scale with raised 7th, exotic Eastern quality

terms.name: melodic minor
terms.definition: Minor scale with raised 6th and 7th ascending

terms.name: Dorian
terms.definition: Mode with W-H-W-W-W-H-W, minor with raised 6th

terms.name: Lydian
terms.definition: Mode with W-W-W-H-W-W-H, major with raised 4th

terms.name: Mixolydian
terms.definition: Mode with W-W-H-W-W-H-W, major with flat 7th

terms.name: Aeolian
terms.definition: Natural minor mode, same as natural minor scale

terms.name: blues scale
terms.definition: Minor pentatonic with added flat5, quintessential blues sound

terms.name: whole tone
terms.definition: Scale of all whole steps, dreamlike and ambiguous

terms.name: octatonic/diminished
terms.definition: Alternating whole and half steps, symmetrical

terms.name: Hungarian minor
terms.definition: Minor scale with raised 4th and 7th, Gypsy flavor

terms.name: double harmonic
terms.definition: Scale with two augmented seconds, Middle Eastern

terms.name: Lydian dominant
terms.definition: Lydian mode with flat 7th, jazz fusion sound

terms.name: Phrygian dominant
terms.definition: Phrygian mode with major 3rd, Middle Eastern

terms.name: acoustic scale
terms.definition: Lydian dominant with natural 7th, overtone series based

terms.name: bebop scale
terms.definition: Eight-note scale adding chromatic passing tone

terms.name: Yo scale
terms.definition: Japanese scale without semitones, bright character

terms.name: enigmatic scale
terms.definition: Unusual scale pattern creating mysterious sound

terms.name: stepwise
terms.definition: Moving by adjacent scale degrees, conjunct motion

terms.name: disjunct
terms.definition: Melodic motion with leaps and skips

terms.name: arch shape
terms.definition: Melody rising then falling in contour

terms.name: inverted arch
terms.definition: Melody falling then rising in contour

terms.name: cantabile
terms.definition: In a singing style, vocal quality

terms.name: recitative
terms.definition: Speech-song style between singing and speaking

terms.name: motivic
terms.definition: Built from short melodic ideas or motives

terms.name: sequenced
terms.definition: Melodic pattern repeated at different pitch levels

terms.name: scalar
terms.definition: Following scale patterns up or down

terms.name: modal
terms.definition: Using scale modes rather than major/minor

terms.name: vibrato
terms.definition: Periodic pitch variation for warmth and expression

terms.name: portamento
terms.definition: Smooth pitch glide from one note to another

terms.name: bend
terms.definition: Pitch alteration, common in guitar and synths

terms.name: flat 3
terms.definition: Lowered third scale degree, minor quality

terms.name: neighbor tone
terms.definition: Non-chord tone stepping away and back

terms.name: appoggiatura
terms.definition: Leaning accented non-chord tone resolving down

terms.name: trill
terms.definition: Rapid alternation between two adjacent notes

terms.name: grace note
terms.definition: Quick unaccented note before main note

terms.name: dominant 7th
terms.definition: Major triad with minor 7th, strong resolution tendency

terms.name: major 7th
terms.definition: Major triad with major 7th, lush and open sound

terms.name: minor 7th
terms.definition: Minor triad with minor 7th, mellow jazz sound

terms.name: minor-major 7th
terms.definition: Minor triad with major 7th, mysterious quality

terms.name: power chord
terms.definition: Root and 5th only, no 3rd, common in rock

terms.name: quartal chord
terms.definition: Chord built from fourths rather than thirds

terms.name: cluster
terms.definition: Chord with adjacent notes creating dissonance

terms.name: polychord
terms.definition: Two or more chords played simultaneously

terms.name: deceptive cadence
terms.definition: Same as interrupted, V to unexpected chord

terms.name: resolution
terms.definition: Satisfying move from tension to stability

terms.name: turnaround
terms.definition: Progression leading back to the beginning

terms.name: stepwise bass
terms.definition: Bass line moving by scale degrees

terms.name: parallel motion
terms.definition: All voices moving in same direction

terms.name: oblique motion
terms.definition: One voice stays while another moves

terms.name: diatonic
terms.definition: Using only notes from the key signature

terms.name: chromatic
terms.definition: Using notes outside the key signature

terms.name: modal
terms.definition: Based on modes rather than major/minor tonality

terms.name: tonal
terms.definition: Organized around a central tonic pitch

terms.name: atonal
terms.definition: Without tonal center, all pitches equal

terms.name: spectral
terms.definition: Based on analysis of sound spectra

terms.name: just intonation
terms.definition: Tuning based on pure acoustic ratios

terms.name: four-on-the-floor
terms.definition: Steady kick drum on every beat, disco/house staple

terms.name: boom-bap
terms.definition: Hip-hop pattern with kick on 1 and 3, snare on 2 and 4

terms.name: trap
terms.definition: Double-time hi-hats with sparse kick and snare

terms.name: breakbeat
terms.definition: Sampled drum break, typically from funk records

terms.name: clave
terms.definition: Afro-Cuban rhythmic pattern, foundation of Latin music

terms.name: son clave
terms.definition: 3-2 or 2-3 pattern used in son music

terms.name: rumba clave
terms.definition: Slightly delayed version used in rumba

terms.name: samba pattern
terms.definition: Brazilian carnival rhythm with surdo drums

terms.name: tresillo
terms.definition: Cuban pattern based on triple subdivision

terms.name: habanera
terms.definition: Cuban rhythm with dotted eighth-sixteenth figure

terms.name: Balkan beat
terms.definition: Asymmetric meters from Eastern European music

terms.name: tight
terms.definition: Controlled, focused sound with no ring

terms.name: dry
terms.definition: Short decay, no reverb or room sound

terms.name: rimshot
terms.definition: Striking drum head and rim together

terms.name: drag
terms.definition: Two quick grace notes before main note

terms.name: buzz roll
terms.definition: Multiple bounce strokes creating sustained sound

terms.name: homorhythmic
terms.definition: All voices move with same rhythm

terms.name: fugal
terms.definition: Imitative polyphonic texture like a fugue

terms.name: breathing
terms.definition: Natural ebb and flow of density

terms.name: interlocking
terms.definition: Parts fitting together like puzzle pieces

terms.name: stacked
terms.definition: Layers built on top of each other

terms.name: melody + countermelody
terms.definition: Main tune with supporting second tune

terms.name: dark
terms.definition: Emphasis on low frequencies, muted highs

terms.name: sawtooth
terms.definition: Wave with all harmonics, bright and buzzy

terms.name: square
terms.definition: Wave with odd harmonics, hollow sound

terms.name: triangle
terms.definition: Wave with odd harmonics, softer than square

terms.name: bit-crushed
terms.definition: Reduced bit depth for lo-fi sound

terms.name: bit-crusher
terms.definition: Reduces bit depth and sample rate

terms.name: tenuto
terms.definition: Held for full value, slightly emphasized

terms.name: slurred
terms.definition: Group of notes played in one bow or breath

terms.name: hammered
terms.definition: Struck like piano or hammered dulcimer

terms.name: da capo aria
terms.definition: ABA form with return to beginning

terms.name: drop
terms.definition: EDM section where full beat enters

terms.name: viola
terms.definition: Alto range bowed string, lower than violin

terms.name: cello
terms.definition: Bass range bowed string, held between knees

terms.name: saxophone (tenor)
terms.definition: Popular jazz sax, lower than alto

terms.name: Roland TB-303
terms.definition: Iconic bass synthesizer that defined the acid house sound with its squelchy, resonant filter

terms.name: Roland TR-808
terms.definition: Legendary drum machine with booming kick and crisp snare, foundational to hip-hop and electronic music

terms.name: Roland TR-909
terms.definition: Drum machine that bridged analog and digital, essential to techno and house music

terms.name: Roland Juno-106
terms.definition: Polyphonic analog synth known for warm pads and iconic 80s chorus sound

terms.name: Roland Jupiter-8
terms.definition: Flagship polyphonic synthesizer with lush, cinematic sound

terms.name: Roland SH-101
terms.definition: Monophonic synth with simple interface, popular for bass and lead sounds

terms.name: Roland D-50
terms.definition: Digital synth famous for ambient pads and new age textures

terms.name: Roland JD-800
terms.definition: Digital synthesizer with hands-on controls and complex layered sounds

terms.name: Roland System-100
terms.definition: Modular analog synthesizer system from the 1970s

terms.name: Roland VP-330
terms.definition: Vocoder and string ensemble synth used by Vangelis and others

terms.name: Roland JX-3P
terms.definition: Programmable polyphonic synth with lush stereo chorus

terms.name: Roland GAIA
terms.definition: Virtual analog synth with three oscillators and modern interface

terms.name: Minimoog Model D
terms.definition: The portable monophonic synth that defined analog bass and lead sounds

terms.name: Moog Modular
terms.definition: Large format modular synthesizer system, the original Moog

terms.name: Moog Sub 37
terms.definition: Paraphonic analog synth with 37 keys and extensive modulation

terms.name: Moog Voyager
terms.definition: Modern reimagining of the Minimoog with MIDI and more

terms.name: Moog Little Phatty
terms.definition: Compact analog synth with Moog ladder filter

terms.name: Moog Mother-32
terms.definition: Semi-modular analog synth with built-in sequencer

terms.name: Moog DFAM
terms.definition: Drummer From Another Mother - percussive analog synth

terms.name: Moog Grandmother
terms.definition: Semi-modular synth with spring reverb and arpeggiator

terms.name: Moog Matriarch
terms.definition: Paraphonic semi-modular with stereo ladder filters

terms.name: Sequential Prophet-5
terms.definition: First fully programmable polyphonic synth, iconic for pads and leads

terms.name: Sequential Prophet-6
terms.definition: Modern reissue of the Prophet-5 with added features

terms.name: Sequential OB-6
terms.definition: Collaboration with Oberheim, rich analog sound

terms.name: Oberheim OB-Xa
terms.definition: Classic polyphonic synth used by Prince and Van Halen

terms.name: Oberheim SEM
terms.definition: Single module expander, the original Oberheim sound

terms.name: Oberheim TVS-1
terms.definition: Two Voice synthesizer with dual SEMs

terms.name: ARP 2600
terms.definition: Semi-modular synth used by Stevie Wonder and Herbie Hancock

terms.name: ARP Odyssey
terms.definition: Duophonic synth, rival to the Minimoog

terms.name: Korg MS-20
terms.definition: Semi-modular synth with aggressive filter, popular in techno

terms.name: Korg Polysix
terms.definition: Affordable six-voice analog with ensemble effect

terms.name: Korg M1
terms.definition: Best-selling digital workstation of all time

terms.name: Yamaha DX7
terms.definition: FM synthesis game-changer, defined 80s pop sounds

terms.name: Yamaha CS-80
terms.definition: Polyphonic analog with polyphonic aftertouch, Blade Runner sound

terms.name: Yamaha Montage
terms.definition: Modern flagship with FM-X and AWM2 engines

terms.name: Yamaha Reface CS
terms.definition: Virtual analog in compact form factor

terms.name: PPG Wave
terms.definition: Wavetable synthesis used by Tangerine Dream

terms.name: Make Noise Maths
terms.definition: Versatile function generator and modulation source

terms.name: ALM Busy Circuits Pam's New Workout
Roland TR-808
terms.definition: Analog drum machine with booming kick, foundational to hip-hop

terms.name: Roland TR-909
terms.definition: Hybrid analog/digital drum machine, essential to techno

terms.name: Roland TR-707
terms.definition: Digital drum machine with PCM samples

terms.name: Roland TR-606
terms.definition: Compact analog drum machine, partner to TB-303

terms.name: Roland TR-8S
terms.definition: Modern AIRA drum machine with sampling

terms.name: Akai MPC60
terms.definition: MIDI Production Center, defined sample-based hip-hop

terms.name: Akai MPC3000
terms.definition: Improved MPC with more memory and features

terms.name: E-Mu SP-1200
terms.definition: 12-bit sampler with gritty sound, classic hip-hop

terms.name: LinnDrum
terms.definition: Roger Linn's drum machine, used on countless 80s hits

terms.name: Linn LM-1
terms.definition: First drum machine to use samples

terms.name: Oberheim DMX
terms.definition: Digital drum machine popular in early hip-hop

terms.name: Yamaha RX5
terms.definition: Digital drum machine with individual outputs

terms.name: Novation Circuit
terms.definition: Groovebox with two synths and drum tracks

terms.name: Fender Stratocaster
terms.definition: Iconic double-cutaway with three single-coil pickups

terms.name: Fender Jazzmaster
terms.definition: Offset body guitar with unique tremolo

terms.name: Gibson ES-335
terms.definition: Semi-hollow body jazz and blues guitar

terms.name: Gibson Explorer
terms.definition: Angular body design, heavy metal icon

terms.name: Maton EBG808
terms.definition: Australian acoustic favored by Tommy Emmanuel

terms.name: Fender Bassman
terms.definition: Originally bass amp, became blues guitar classic

terms.name: Marshall 1959 Super Lead
terms.definition: The Plexi, defined rock guitar tone

terms.name: Vox AC30
terms.definition: British chime, Beatles and U2 sound

terms.name: Vox AC15
terms.definition: Lower wattage version with same character

terms.name: Orange AD30
terms.definition: British tone with distinctive orange tolex

terms.name: Ableton Live
terms.definition: DAW designed for live performance and electronic production

terms.name: FL Studio
terms.definition: Pattern-based DAW popular with hip-hop and EDM producers

terms.name: Logic Pro
terms.definition: Apple's professional DAW with extensive instruments

terms.name: Cubase
terms.definition: Long-running DAW with strong MIDI features

terms.name: Reason
terms.definition: Virtual rack of instruments and effects

terms.name: GarageBand
terms.definition: Entry-level DAW for Mac and iOS

terms.name: Massive
terms.definition: Native Instruments wavetable synth, dubstep staple

terms.name: Pigments
terms.definition: Arturia hybrid wavetable and analog synth

terms.name: Analog Lab
terms.definition: Arturia collection of vintage synth emulations

terms.name: Spire
terms.definition: Polyphonic software synthesizer by Reveal Sound

terms.name: Amen Break
terms.definition: Most sampled drum break of all time, from Amen Brother

terms.name: Funky Drummer
terms.definition: James Brown break, foundational to hip-hop

terms.name: Think Break
terms.definition: Lyn Collins break, used in countless jungle tracks

terms.name: Apache Break
terms.definition: Incredible Bongo Band, early hip-hop staple

terms.name: Ashley's Roachclip
terms.definition: Soul Searchers break with iconic open hi-hat

terms.name: It's a New Day
terms.definition: Skull Snaps break, funky and tight

terms.name: Tramp
terms.definition: Lowell Fulson break, simple and effective

terms.name: Soul Pride
terms.definition: James Brown break with signature snare

terms.name: Yeah Boy
terms.definition: Pitched vocal stab from Lyn Collins

terms.name: Wilhelm Scream
terms.definition: Iconic stock sound effect used in hundreds of films

terms.name: Inception Horn
terms.definition: BRAAAM sound from Hans Zimmer score
